steps: The triangle has three sides: one labeled $x$ inches and two labeled $y$ inches each.
Perimeter = sum of all three sides: $x + y + y = x + 2y$.

• Choice A — '$x+y=69$': Accounts for only two sides (one $x$ and one $y$), missing the second $y$ side. ✗
  • Choice B — '$2x+y=69$': Would require two sides of length $x$, but the triangle has only one. ✗
  • Choice C — '$x+2y=69$': One side of $x$ plus two sides of $y$ gives $x + 2y = 69$. ✓
  • Choice D — '$2x+2y=69$': Would require two sides of length $x$, but the triangle has only one. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
